text: RBI kept repo rate unchanged at 6.50% in December 2024 MPC meeting. Governor Shaktikanta Das continued pause on rate changes citing inflation concerns.
RBI kept repo rate unchanged at 6.50% in October 2024. Changed stance from 'withdrawal of accommodation' to 'neutral'.
RBI maintained repo rate at 6.50% in February 2024. Sixth consecutive pause.
RBI raised repo rate by 25 basis points to 6.50% in April 2023. This was the last rate hike in the tightening cycle.
RBI raised the repo rate to 6.25% in February 2023, a 25 bps hike from 6.00%.
RBI raised repo rate by 35 bps to 6.25% in December 2022.
RBI began tightening cycle with surprise 40 bps hike to 4.40% in May 2022, responding to rising inflation after COVID.
RBI cut repo rate to historic low of 4.00% during COVID-19 pandemic to support the economy.
RBI cut repo rate to 6.25% in February 2019, beginning an easing cycle under Governor Shaktikanta Das.
RBI raised repo rate to 6.50% in August 2018 under Governor Urjit Patel, citing inflation concerns.
RBI cut repo rate to 6.25% in October 2016 under Governor Urjit Patel.
